content.full_text:
August 6, 1990

Jeanne Pritchard, Chief
Technical Analysis and Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Informal Advice

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

Pursuant to FPPC Regulation 18329 (c), I would like to request informal advice regarding the following situation.

Assume that a labor union sponsors a recipient committee in accordance with FPPC regulation 18419 for the purpose of supporting and opposing candidates for elective office. Assume further that the union desires to make a contribution to a committee supporting a ballot measure and for tax reasons does not wish to make the contribution from its sponsored committee.

Assume the union makes the ballot measure contribution from its general treasury funds. May it disclose the contribution on its sponsored committee's campaign report on the allocation page with a notation that the expenditure was made from the sponsoring organization's general funds?

Assume the contribution is disclosed on the sponsored committee's campaign report as noted above. Would the union be required to file a separate major donor report if such contributions exceeded $10,000.00 in a calendar year?

Assume the contribution is disclosed on the sponsored committee's campaign report as noted above. Would the union be required to register as a recipient committee pursuant to Government Code §82013 if the original contribution was at least $1,000.00 and the union made a subsequent contribution the following calendar year from its general treasury funds? Assume the union would also report the subsequent contribution

Jeanne Pritchard  
August 6, 1990  
Page 2

on the sponsored committee's allocation page of its campaign report in the second year.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO/k1
